prompt.content:
It turns out that all assets should have been depreciated over a period 2 years shorter than they are currently. We have also received final (updated attached) list of sales shipped in 2021-23. What is the total updated cumulative tax exposure as of December 31, 2023, given these changes? Please use data from the audited Redwood Tax Exposure model. Please assume that penalties are assessed on Dec 31 of the year they relate to, and that interest (both for unpaid tax and penalties) starts accumulating from December 31 of the year it relates to.

Please state your final answer in $ rounding it to 1 decimal place.

rubrics.name: States that the total updated cumulative tax exposure as of December 31, 2023,... (ver_e5fd1f4b75fa4377a43dc6f8386d99c1)

messages.content: States that the total updated cumulative tax exposure as of December 31, 2023, after adjusting for the shortened depreciation period and the updated sales data, is $2,125,508.4 (acceptable range $2,104,253.3 to $2,146,763.5).

golden_responses.golden_text_response: The total updated cumulative tax exposure as of December 31, 2023, after adjusting for the shortened depreciation period and the updated sales data, is $2,125,508.4
